# WP-E — Export Pipeline & Interoperability

## Context

You are an orchestrating engineer on the bim-ai repository (`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`).
bim-ai is a browser-based BIM authoring tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest).

Repo layout (critical paths):
- `packages/web/src/` — main web app
- `packages/core/src/` — shared types (Element, XY, Level, Material, etc.) — study these thoroughly; export code must handle these types
- `packages/web/src/schedules/SchedulePanel.tsx` — schedule view (add CSV export button here)
- `packages/web/src/workspace/sheets/SheetCanvas.tsx` — sheet canvas (add PDF export trigger here)
- `packages/web/src/commandQueue.ts` — command dispatch
- `packages/web/src/jobs/` — check what exists for background jobs/export tasks

There is currently NO export directory. You will create:
- `packages/web/src/export/` — new directory for all export logic

Architecture patterns:
- Exports are triggered from the UI, processed in a Web Worker (to avoid blocking the main thread), and result in a file download via `URL.createObjectURL`.
- The project model is accessed via a Zustand store — read the existing store pattern (check `packages/web/src/state/` or wherever the project model lives) to understand how to read elements.
- Use established npm packages where available: `ifc.js` / `web-ifc` for IFC, `dxf-writer` for DXF, browser's native `Blob` for CSV/PDF.
- Prettier runs automatically. Tests: `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web`.

## Safe Parallel Work Rules

1. `git pull --rebase origin main` before starting each sub-task.
2. Commit + push after every completed sub-feature.
3. `git pull --rebase origin main && git push origin main` after each push.
4. The export directory is new — no conflicts with other WPs expected. `toolRegistry.ts` is not touched by this WP.
5. Do NOT touch: stair/ramp files, annotation tools, phase dialogs, structural, massing, view/sheet rendering. Those belong to other WPs.

## Your Mission

Implement the full export and interoperability pipeline. This covers tracker Chapter 12 items and parts of Chapter 13. These are P1 gaps that block professional handoff workflows. Dispatch sub-agents for independent export formats.

---

### Sub-task E1: IFC 2x3 Export

IFC (Industry Foundation Classes) is the standard open BIM exchange format. This is a critical professional feature.

Package recommendation: `web-ifc` (npm: `web-ifc`) or `@thatopen/ifc-fragment` — evaluate and pick the one best suited for write-only use in a browser.

Create `packages/web/src/export/ifcExporter.ts`:

Map bim-ai element types to IFC entities:
- `wall` → `IfcWall` + `IfcWallStandardCase` (use IfcWallStandardCase for layered walls)
- `door` → `IfcDoor`
- `window` → `IfcWindow`
- `floor` → `IfcSlab` (PredefinedType=FLOOR)
- `roof` → `IfcRoof` / `IfcSlab` (PredefinedType=ROOF)
- `stair` → `IfcStair`
- `railing` → `IfcRailing`
- `column` → `IfcColumn`
- `beam` → `IfcBeam`
- `ceiling` → `IfcSlab` (PredefinedType=CEILING)
- `room` → `IfcSpace`
- Level → `IfcBuildingStorey`
- Project → `IfcProject` + `IfcBuilding` + `IfcSite`

Geometry export:
- Walls: export as `IfcExtrudedAreaSolid` (rectangle profile extruded to wall height). Layer boundaries exported as `IfcMaterialLayerSetUsage`.
- Openings (doors/windows): export as `IfcOpeningElement` with `IfcRelVoidsElement` relationship to host wall.
- Floors/roofs: export as `IfcFacetedBrep` from the Three.js mesh geometry (triangle soup).
- Rooms: export as `IfcSpace` with floor area property.

Properties:
- Each element gets an `IfcPropertySet` "Pset_WallCommon" / "Pset_DoorCommon" etc. with standard IFC properties.
- Custom bim-ai properties go in a "Pset_BimAiCustom" property set.

UI integration:
- File menu "Export" → "IFC 2x3…" opens a dialog with options: include structure, include MEP, coordinate system (local/WGS84).
- Export runs in a Web Worker. Progress shown in a small toast.
- Result: browser download of `ProjectName.ifc`.

Tests:
- `ifcExporter.test.ts`: export a simple model with 2 walls + 1 door, verify the output string contains `IFCWALL`, `IFCDOOR`, `IFCOPENINGELEMENT`.
- Verify the exported IFC can be parsed back by web-ifc (round-trip parse check).

### Sub-task E2: DWG / DXF Export

DWG/DXF export for handing off to AutoCAD users.

Package: `dxf-writer` (npm) — outputs DXF format which can be opened by most CAD tools and saved-as DWG by AutoCAD.

Create `packages/web/src/export/dxfExporter.ts`:

Layer mapping (Revit-compatible layer names):
- Walls → layer `A-WALL`
- Doors → `A-DOOR`
- Windows → `A-GLAZ`
- Columns → `A-COLS`
- Stairs → `A-STRS`
- Annotations → `A-ANNO`
- Dimensions → `A-ANNO-DIMS`
- Rooms → `A-AREA`
- Levels (as horizontal lines) → `A-FLOR-LEVL`
- Grid lines → `S-GRID`
- Reference planes → `A-REFP`

For each plan view being exported:
- Walls: export as pairs of parallel polylines (inner face + outer face) or as a closed hatched polyline for layered walls.
- Doors: export as arc (door swing) + line (door leaf).
- Windows: export as 3 lines (frame outline, glass line).
- Rooms: export as a closed polyline (room boundary) on the A-AREA layer.
- Dimensions: export as DXF DIMENSION entities.
- Grids: CIRCLE + LINE entities.
- Text annotations: TEXT entities.

UI:
- File menu "Export" → "DXF/DWG…" → dialog: pick views to export (one DXF per view, or all in one file using blocks per view), units (mm/m), layer naming (Revit-compatible or custom).
- Download as `ProjectName_Grundriss_EG.dxf` (one per exported view).

Tests:
- `dxfExporter.test.ts`: export a plan with 4 walls, verify the DXF string contains `POLYLINE` entities on layer `A-WALL`.
- Test layer assignment for door and window elements.

### Sub-task E3: CSV Export from Schedule Views

Each schedule view in `schedules/SchedulePanel.tsx` should be downloadable as a CSV.

- Add a "Download CSV" button to `SchedulePanel.tsx` (and `ScheduleView.tsx`).
- Create `packages/web/src/export/csvExporter.ts`: takes a schedule definition (columns + rows from the schedule renderer) and produces a UTF-8 CSV string.
- The CSV respects column order, column header names, and numeric formatting (units).
- Trigger: button click → `csvExporter.exportSchedule(scheduleData)` → `URL.createObjectURL(new Blob([csv]))` → `<a download>` click.
- Also support "Copy to Clipboard" (navigator.clipboard.writeText) as an alternative.
- All existing schedule types should export: room finish schedules, stair schedules, opening schedules, level datum schedules.

Tests:
- `csvExporter.test.ts`: pass a mock schedule with 3 columns and 5 rows, verify output has correct header row and 5 data rows.

### Sub-task E4: PDF Export from Sheets (polished)

PDF export of sheet views should be production-quality.

Approach:
- Use `jspdf` (npm: `jspdf`) + `html2canvas` or direct canvas capture.
- `packages/web/src/export/pdfExporter.ts`:
  - Takes a sheet ID. Renders the sheet canvas to an off-screen canvas at high resolution (≥300 DPI equivalent for print quality).
  - Adds the rendered canvas as an image page in a jsPDF document.
  - Supports multi-sheet export: "Export All Sheets" creates a multi-page PDF.
- Sheet size: read from the sheet's paper size property (A4, A3, A2, A1, A0 — store as sheet property).
- UI: "Print/Export PDF" in the sheet canvas toolbar → opens a dialog: single sheet or all sheets, paper size confirmation, DPI setting (72/150/300).
- Output: browser download of `ProjectName_Sheets.pdf`.

Tests:
- `pdfExporter.test.ts`: mock a sheet canvas, verify the PDF blob has non-zero size and the correct page count.

### Sub-task E5: Linked Model (Basic Read-Only)

A "linked model" lets one bim-ai project reference another as a background underlay — useful for coordination between architects and structural/MEP consultants.

This is a simplified implementation (read-only):

Data model:
- `linked_model` element: `{ id, projectUrl: string, insertionXMm, insertionYMm, rotationDeg, isVisible, isGhosted }`
- The `projectUrl` is a URL to another bim-ai project (same cloud backend).

UI:
- "Insert → Link bim-ai Model": opens a project picker showing other projects the user has access to.
- Inserted as a `linked_model` element.
- In plan view: linked model elements render ghosted (grey, low opacity) — reuse `linkedGhosting.ts` which already exists.
- In 3D: same ghosted treatment.
- Inspector: linked model shows URL, offset, rotation, visibility toggle, "Unload" (hides it temporarily) and "Remove" buttons.
- The linked model is NOT editable — elements inside it cannot be selected or modified.

Tests:
- `linkedModelGhosting.test.ts`: verify linked elements render with ghost opacity.

### Sub-task E6: DXF Underlay Completion Pass

The existing `dxfUnderlay.ts` partially implements DXF import. Complete it:

- Currently only basic line entities are imported. Add support for: ARC, CIRCLE, POLYLINE, LWPOLYLINE, TEXT, MTEXT, INSERT (block references), HATCH (convert to polygon).
- Layer visibility: each DXF layer can be toggled visible/hidden from a panel.
- Layer colours: respect DXF layer colours.
- Underlay opacity control: slider in the inspector for the underlay element.
- "Snap to Underlay": a toggle that makes snap engine snap to underlay geometry (endpoint/midpoint of underlay lines).
- Tests: `dxfUnderlay.arc.test.ts` — verify ARC entities are parsed and rendered as canvas arcs.

---

## Definition of Done

For each sub-task:
- TypeScript compiles without errors
- ≥2 unit tests per new module
- Export functions produce correct output (validated by parsing or content checks)
- UI trigger present and functional
- No regressions in existing schedule/sheet tests
- Tracker entries updated in `spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md`

21	- Project-level settings (like units, project info) live as special model elements or in a project settings object. Study how `osm/project.ts` stores georef data to understand the pattern.
22	- Phase filter: `phaseFilter.ts` defines `PhaseFilter` type. Elements have `phaseId?: string`. The filter controls what renders.
23	- Semantic commands: `{ type: 'createPhase', name }`, `{ type: 'setElementPhase', elementId, phaseId }`, etc.
24	- Prettier runs automatically. Tests: `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web`.

34	## Your Mission

37	
38	---
39	
40	### Sub-task F1: Full Phase Management Dialog
41	
42	Currently phases can filter what renders, but phases cannot be created, deleted, or renamed by the user. Implement a complete phase management system.

70	### Sub-task F2: Phase Graphic Overrides
71	
72	Revit shows elements from different phases in different graphic styles in the same view:
73	- **Existing** (Bestand): shown in grey halftone lines, no fill
74	- **Demolished** (Abriss): shown as dashed, with a cross-hatch fill in demolition views
75	- **New Construction** (Neubau): shown in normal black lines with material fills

77	This is controlled by the active "phase filter" on the view (e.g. "New Construction" view shows Bestand elements as grey, Abriss elements as dashed, Neubau as normal).
78	
79	Implementation:
80	
81	Per-view phase filter settings (add to `plan_view` element):
82	```ts
83	phaseFilterMode: 'new_construction' | 'demolition' | 'existing' | 'all'
84	phaseGraphicOverrides: {
85	  existing: { lineStyle: 'solid' | 'dashed', opacity: number, fillColor: string | null },
86	  demo: { lineStyle: 'solid' | 'dashed', opacity: number, fillColor: string | null, showCrossHatch: boolean },
87	  new: { lineStyle: 'solid', opacity: 1, fillColor: null },
88	}
89	```
90	
91	Plan projection: pass `phaseGraphicOverrides` to `planProjection.ts`. When rendering a wall/floor, look up its phase override and apply the corresponding line style and opacity.
92	
93	3D viewport: pass phase overrides to the material system. Existing elements get a grey material override; demolished elements get a semi-transparent dashed material (use a custom `proceduralMaterials.ts` variant).
94	
95	Preset phase filter modes:
96	- "New Construction": Bestand=grey/thin, Abriss=not shown, Neubau=normal
97	- "Demolition Plan": Bestand=grey, Abriss=dashed+crosshatch, Neubau=not shown
98	- "As Built": all phases shown in their normal graphic
99	- "Existing": only Bestand shown in normal graphic
100	
101	UI: add "Phase Filter" dropdown to `PlanViewHeader.tsx` (next to the existing phase selector). Each view can have its own phase filter mode.
102

104	- `phaseFilter.graphicOverrides.test.ts`: apply "Demolition Plan" filter, verify a demo-phase wall gets dashed line style
105	- `planProjection.phaseFilter.test.ts`: verify existing-phase walls render with reduced opacity
106	
107	### Sub-task F3: Project Information Dialog
108	
109	Revit's Projektinformationen dialog stores metadata: project number, project name, project address, project status, client name, author, check date, issue date.
110	
111	This data feeds title blocks on sheets.
112	
113	Data model — project settings object (extend what `osm/project.ts` already stores):
114	```ts
115	projectInfo: {

117	  projectName: string,

131	- Opened from workspace settings or a "Project Info…" menu item.
132	- Form with all fields above (text inputs + date pickers for dates).
133	- Save button dispatches `updateProjectInfo`.
134	
135	Sheet title block integration:
136	- The sheet canvas's title block area (bottom right of each sheet) renders project info fields.
137	- Specifically: project name, project number, client, author, issue date, sheet number, sheet name, revision info.
138	- Update `workspace/sheets/SheetCanvas.tsx` to read from the project info store and render these fields.
139	- If a field is empty, show a greyed-out placeholder text.
140	
141	Tests:
142	- `projectInfoDialog.test.tsx`: fill in project name + number, verify the store updates
143	- `sheetTitleBlock.test.tsx`: verify project name appears in the sheet footer area
144	
145	### Sub-task F4: Project Units Dialog
146	
147	Currently bim-ai uses a default unit system. Implement user-configurable units.
148	
149	Data model — `projectUnits` in project settings:
150	```ts
151	projectUnits: {
152	  lengthUnit: 'mm' | 'm' | 'cm' | 'ft' | 'in' | 'ft-in',
153	  areaUnit: 'm2' | 'ft2',
154	  volumeUnit: 'm3' | 'ft3',
155	  angleUnit: 'degrees' | 'radians',
156	  decimalSymbol: '.' | ',',
157	  numberGrouping: 'space' | 'comma' | 'none',
158	}
159	```
160	
161	UI — `packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectUnitsDialog.tsx`:
162	- Dropdown for each unit category.
163	- Live preview showing how a sample value (e.g. 3500mm) would display in the selected format.
164	- On Save: dispatches `{ type: 'updateProjectUnits', units: ProjectUnits }`.
165	
166	Apply units everywhere:
167	- All dimension text values: reformat from internal mm storage to the display unit.
168	- Property inspector numeric values: display in project units.
169	- Temp dimension tooltips: display in project units.
170	- Create `packages/web/src/lib/formatUnit.ts`: a pure function `formatLength(valueInMm: number, units: ProjectUnits): string` — use this everywhere. Tests required.
171	
172	Tests:
173	- `formatUnit.test.ts`: 3500mm → "3500 mm" (metric), "3.500 m" (meters), "11'-5 13/16\"" (ft-in)
174	- `projectUnitsDialog.test.tsx`: switch to feet, verify inspector shows ft values
175	
176	### Sub-task F5: Project Templates at Project Creation
177	
178	When creating a new project, Revit asks for a template: None / BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau (vereinfacht) / BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau / BIM Gebäudetechnik.
179

181	- Level structure (EG, OG1, OG2, Dach for residential)

189	- Template: `'minimal'` (1 level, basic views), `'residential'` (4 levels, full view set, 3 phases), `'commercial'` (multi-level, MEP-ready).
190	- Project creation dialog: add a template picker (step 1 of the new project wizard).
191	- On project creation with a template: dispatch a batch of `createLevel`, `createView`, `createPhase`, `createWallType` commands from the template definition.

193	Tests:

195	
196	### Sub-task F6: Project Position / True North
197	
198	Complete the project position workflow from the tracker (partially done in OSM):
199	
200	- Revit's "Project Position" lets you set the angle between "Project North" (the drawing's up direction) and "True North" (geographic north).
201	- This offset is stored as `projectNorthAngleDeg` in project settings.
202	- When printing/exporting, views can be set to "True North" or "Project North" orientation.

204	- Update `sunStore.ts` to use `projectNorthAngleDeg` when computing sun position (already wired for geographic bearing — make sure project north offset is applied).
205	- UI: in project settings, a numeric input "True North angle" (degrees clockwise from project north).

207	### Sub-task F7: Visibility / Graphics Override Dialog (V/G)
208	
209	Revit's "Visibility/Graphics Overrides" (keyboard shortcut VV or VG) is the primary per-view override system.

211	Create a simplified version:

214	- Shortcut `VV` opens it from any plan/section view.
215	- Shows a table of model categories (Walls, Doors, Windows, Floors, Roofs, Stairs, Columns, Grids, Reference Planes, Rooms, etc.).
216	- Each row: category name, checkbox (Visible), Line Colour button, Line Weight dropdown, Fill Pattern dropdown, Halftone checkbox.
217	- Overrides stored in the view element: `visibilityOverrides: { [category: string]: { visible: boolean, lineColor: string, lineWeight: number, fillPattern: string | null, halftone: boolean } }`.

223	Tests:
224	- `visibilityGraphicsDialog.test.tsx`: hide the "Grids" category, verify grid elements are not rendered
225	- `planProjection.visibilityOverrides.test.ts`: apply halftone override to walls, verify opacity

# WP-F — Phases, Project Configuration & Templates

## Context

You are an orchestrating engineer on the bim-ai repository (`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`).
bim-ai is a browser-based BIM authoring tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest).

Repo layout (critical paths):
- `packages/web/src/viewport/phaseFilter.ts` — existing phase filter logic (start here)
- `packages/web/src/plan/PhaseDropdown.tsx` — existing phase selector in plan header
- `packages/web/src/plan/PlanViewHeader.tsx` — plan header (add view range, phase graphic buttons)
- `packages/web/src/plan/planProjection.ts` — plan rendering (wire phase graphic overrides here)
- `packages/web/src/viewport/renderStyles.ts` — 3D render styles (wire phase colours here)
- `packages/web/src/workspace/Workspace.tsx` — top-level workspace (add project info dialog trigger)
- `packages/web/src/workspace/project/` — check what project settings UI exists
- `packages/web/src/osm/project.ts` — georef / project settings
- `packages/web/src/onboarding/` — project setup onboarding steps (add units step)
- `packages/core/src/` — shared types

Architecture patterns:
- Project-level settings (like units, project info) live as special model elements or in a project settings object. Study how `osm/project.ts` stores georef data to understand the pattern.
- Phase filter: `phaseFilter.ts` defines `PhaseFilter` type. Elements have `phaseId?: string`. The filter controls what renders.
- Semantic commands: `{ type: 'createPhase', name }`, `{ type: 'setElementPhase', elementId, phaseId }`, etc.
- Prettier runs automatically. Tests: `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web`.

## Safe Parallel Work Rules

1. `git pull --rebase origin main` before starting each sub-task.
2. Commit + push after every completed sub-feature.
3. `git pull --rebase origin main && git push origin main` after each push.
4. Do NOT touch: stair/ramp files, annotation tools, export pipeline, structural/massing, groups, view/sheet rendering systems. Those belong to other WPs.
5. `toolRegistry.ts` is NOT modified by this WP.

## Your Mission

Implement full phase management, project information dialog, project units, and project template selection. These cover tracker Chapters 2.8, 1.5, parts of 1.6, and the "Verwalten" register from Chapter 1.

---

### Sub-task F1: Full Phase Management Dialog

Currently phases can filter what renders, but phases cannot be created, deleted, or renamed by the user. Implement a complete phase management system.

Data model additions:
- `phase` model element: `{ id, name, sequenceNumber, description }`
- Default project phases: `[{ id: 'existing', name: 'Bestand', sequenceNumber: 1 }, { id: 'demo', name: 'Abriss', sequenceNumber: 2 }, { id: 'new', name: 'Neubau', sequenceNumber: 3 }]`
- Each element already has `phaseId?: string` — elements without a phaseId are treated as `'new'`.

New semantic commands:
- `{ type: 'createPhase', name, sequenceNumber }` — adds a new phase
- `{ type: 'deletePhase', phaseId }` — removes a phase; elements of that phase are re-assigned to the adjacent phase
- `{ type: 'renamePhase', phaseId, name }` — renames
- `{ type: 'reorderPhase', phaseId, newSequenceNumber }` — change sequence
- `{ type: 'setElementPhase', elementId, phaseId }` — assign element to phase

UI — "Manage Phases" dialog (`packages/web/src/phases/PhaseManagerDialog.tsx`):
- Opened from: workspace settings menu or "Verwalten → Phasen" equivalent.
- Shows a table of phases with columns: Sequence, Name, Description.
- Rows can be added, deleted, renamed, reordered via drag-and-drop.
- Warning when deleting a phase that has elements: "N elements will be moved to [previous phase]".

Phase assignment in inspector:
- When any element is selected, the inspector shows a "Phase" dropdown (all available phases).
- Changing it dispatches `setElementPhase`.

Phase assignment in plan view:
- The existing `PhaseDropdown.tsx` controls which phase is "active" (new elements placed go into this phase).
- The active phase is shown in the plan header.

### Sub-task F2: Phase Graphic Overrides

Revit shows elements from different phases in different graphic styles in the same view:
- **Existing** (Bestand): shown in grey halftone lines, no fill
- **Demolished** (Abriss): shown as dashed, with a cross-hatch fill in demolition views
- **New Construction** (Neubau): shown in normal black lines with material fills

This is controlled by the active "phase filter" on the view (e.g. "New Construction" view shows Bestand elements as grey, Abriss elements as dashed, Neubau as normal).

Implementation:

Per-view phase filter settings (add to `plan_view` element):
```ts
phaseFilterMode: 'new_construction' | 'demolition' | 'existing' | 'all'
phaseGraphicOverrides: {
  existing: { lineStyle: 'solid' | 'dashed', opacity: number, fillColor: string | null },
  demo: { lineStyle: 'solid' | 'dashed', opacity: number, fillColor: string | null, showCrossHatch: boolean },
  new: { lineStyle: 'solid', opacity: 1, fillColor: null },
}
```

Plan projection: pass `phaseGraphicOverrides` to `planProjection.ts`. When rendering a wall/floor, look up its phase override and apply the corresponding line style and opacity.

3D viewport: pass phase overrides to the material system. Existing elements get a grey material override; demolished elements get a semi-transparent dashed material (use a custom `proceduralMaterials.ts` variant).

Preset phase filter modes:
- "New Construction": Bestand=grey/thin, Abriss=not shown, Neubau=normal
- "Demolition Plan": Bestand=grey, Abriss=dashed+crosshatch, Neubau=not shown
- "As Built": all phases shown in their normal graphic
- "Existing": only Bestand shown in normal graphic

UI: add "Phase Filter" dropdown to `PlanViewHeader.tsx` (next to the existing phase selector). Each view can have its own phase filter mode.

Tests:
- `phaseFilter.graphicOverrides.test.ts`: apply "Demolition Plan" filter, verify a demo-phase wall gets dashed line style
- `planProjection.phaseFilter.test.ts`: verify existing-phase walls render with reduced opacity

### Sub-task F3: Project Information Dialog

Revit's Projektinformationen dialog stores metadata: project number, project name, project address, project status, client name, author, check date, issue date.

This data feeds title blocks on sheets.

Data model — project settings object (extend what `osm/project.ts` already stores):
```ts
projectInfo: {
  projectNumber: string,
  projectName: string,
  projectAddress: string,
  projectStatus: string,
  clientName: string,
  authorName: string,
  issueDate: string,       // ISO date
  checkDate: string,       // ISO date
  description: string,
}
```

Semantic command: `{ type: 'updateProjectInfo', patch: Partial<ProjectInfo> }`

UI — `packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectInfoDialog.tsx`:
- Opened from workspace settings or a "Project Info…" menu item.
- Form with all fields above (text inputs + date pickers for dates).
- Save button dispatches `updateProjectInfo`.

Sheet title block integration:
- The sheet canvas's title block area (bottom right of each sheet) renders project info fields.
- Specifically: project name, project number, client, author, issue date, sheet number, sheet name, revision info.
- Update `workspace/sheets/SheetCanvas.tsx` to read from the project info store and render these fields.
- If a field is empty, show a greyed-out placeholder text.

Tests:
- `projectInfoDialog.test.tsx`: fill in project name + number, verify the store updates
- `sheetTitleBlock.test.tsx`: verify project name appears in the sheet footer area

### Sub-task F4: Project Units Dialog

Currently bim-ai uses a default unit system. Implement user-configurable units.

Data model — `projectUnits` in project settings:
```ts
projectUnits: {
  lengthUnit: 'mm' | 'm' | 'cm' | 'ft' | 'in' | 'ft-in',
  areaUnit: 'm2' | 'ft2',
  volumeUnit: 'm3' | 'ft3',
  angleUnit: 'degrees' | 'radians',
  decimalSymbol: '.' | ',',
  numberGrouping: 'space' | 'comma' | 'none',
}
```

UI — `packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectUnitsDialog.tsx`:
- Dropdown for each unit category.
- Live preview showing how a sample value (e.g. 3500mm) would display in the selected format.
- On Save: dispatches `{ type: 'updateProjectUnits', units: ProjectUnits }`.

Apply units everywhere:
- All dimension text values: reformat from internal mm storage to the display unit.
- Property inspector numeric values: display in project units.
- Temp dimension tooltips: display in project units.
- Create `packages/web/src/lib/formatUnit.ts`: a pure function `formatLength(valueInMm: number, units: ProjectUnits): string` — use this everywhere. Tests required.

Tests:
- `formatUnit.test.ts`: 3500mm → "3500 mm" (metric), "3.500 m" (meters), "11'-5 13/16\"" (ft-in)
- `projectUnitsDialog.test.tsx`: switch to feet, verify inspector shows ft values

### Sub-task F5: Project Templates at Project Creation

When creating a new project, Revit asks for a template: None / BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau (vereinfacht) / BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau / BIM Gebäudetechnik.

Templates pre-populate the project with:
- Level structure (EG, OG1, OG2, Dach for residential)
- View structure (plan views per level, 3D view, 4 elevation views)
- Phase structure (Bestand, Abriss, Neubau)
- Wall type catalog entries
- Default dimension styles

Implement:
- `packages/web/src/onboarding/projectTemplates.ts`: define template definitions as static data objects.
- Template: `'minimal'` (1 level, basic views), `'residential'` (4 levels, full view set, 3 phases), `'commercial'` (multi-level, MEP-ready).
- Project creation dialog: add a template picker (step 1 of the new project wizard).
- On project creation with a template: dispatch a batch of `createLevel`, `createView`, `createPhase`, `createWallType` commands from the template definition.

Tests:
- `projectTemplates.test.ts`: apply "residential" template, verify 4 levels + plan views + phases are created

### Sub-task F6: Project Position / True North

Complete the project position workflow from the tracker (partially done in OSM):

- Revit's "Project Position" lets you set the angle between "Project North" (the drawing's up direction) and "True North" (geographic north).
- This offset is stored as `projectNorthAngleDeg` in project settings.
- When printing/exporting, views can be set to "True North" or "Project North" orientation.
- In plan view header: a "True North" toggle that rotates the view by `projectNorthAngleDeg`.
- Update `sunStore.ts` to use `projectNorthAngleDeg` when computing sun position (already wired for geographic bearing — make sure project north offset is applied).
- UI: in project settings, a numeric input "True North angle" (degrees clockwise from project north).

### Sub-task F7: Visibility / Graphics Override Dialog (V/G)

Revit's "Visibility/Graphics Overrides" (keyboard shortcut VV or VG) is the primary per-view override system.

Create a simplified version:

UI — `packages/web/src/workspace/visibilityGraphicsDialog/VisibilityGraphicsDialog.tsx`:
- Shortcut `VV` opens it from any plan/section view.
- Shows a table of model categories (Walls, Doors, Windows, Floors, Roofs, Stairs, Columns, Grids, Reference Planes, Rooms, etc.).
- Each row: category name, checkbox (Visible), Line Colour button, Line Weight dropdown, Fill Pattern dropdown, Halftone checkbox.
- Overrides stored in the view element: `visibilityOverrides: { [category: string]: { visible: boolean, lineColor: string, lineWeight: number, fillPattern: string | null, halftone: boolean } }`.

Plan projection: thread `visibilityOverrides` through `planProjection.ts`. For each element, look up its category override and apply to the Canvas 2D drawing calls.

Start with just: Visible toggle (most important), Line Colour, Halftone. Pattern and weight overrides are bonus.

Tests:
- `visibilityGraphicsDialog.test.tsx`: hide the "Grids" category, verify grid elements are not rendered
- `planProjection.visibilityOverrides.test.ts`: apply halftone override to walls, verify opacity

---

## Definition of Done

For each sub-task:
- TypeScript compiles without errors
- ≥2 unit tests per new module
- Feature is accessible from the workspace UI
- No regressions in existing phase/plan projection tests
- Tracker entry updated in `spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md`

1	# WP-G — Structural Completion & Conceptual Massing
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	You are an orchestrating engineer on the bim-ai repository (`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`).
6	bim-ai is a browser-based BIM authoring tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest).
7	
8	Repo layout (critical paths):
9	- `packages/web/src/tools/toolRegistry.ts` — ToolId union + TOOL_REGISTRY (add: brace, beam-system, column-at-grids, mass tools)
10	- `packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.ts` — 3D mesh dispatcher (add entries for new element types)
11	- `packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.mass.ts` — EXISTING mass mesh builder (study this first)
12	- `packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.mass.test.ts` — mass tests
13	- `packages/web/src/plan/planProjection.ts` — 2D projection (add mass footprints, beam system lines)
14	- `packages/web/src/plan/planElementMeshBuilders.ts` — 2D plan element renderers
15	- `packages/core/src/` — shared types (Element, XY, etc.)
16	
17	Architecture patterns:
18	- All new elements follow the same pattern: define element shape in core types, implement 3D mesh builder in `viewport/meshBuilders.<type>.ts`, implement 2D plan symbol in `plan/<type>PlanSymbol.ts`, add to `meshBuilders.ts` dispatcher and `planProjection.ts`.
19	- Semantic commands: study `{ type: 'createWall', ... }` and `{ type: 'createRoof', ... }` for command shape conventions.
20	- Structural elements have `structuralRole: 'structural' | 'non-structural'` on their data.
21	- Prettier runs automatically. Tests: `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web`.
22	
23	## Safe Parallel Work Rules
24	
25	1. `git pull --rebase origin main` before starting each sub-task.
26	2. Commit + push after every completed sub-feature.
27	3. `git pull --rebase origin main && git push origin main` after each push.
28	4. Do NOT touch: annotation/dim files, stair/ramp (WP-C), export (WP-E), phase dialogs (WP-F), groups (WP-B), view/sheet rendering (WP-D).
29	5. `toolRegistry.ts` is shared — always rebase before editing.
30	
31	## Your Mission
32	
33	Implement structural completion (beam systems, braces, column grid placement, sloped columns) and the conceptual massing / top-down design workflow. Covers tracker Chapters 9 and 11 entirely, plus parts of 8. Dispatch sub-agents for independent sub-tasks.
34	
35	---
36	
37	### Sub-task G1: Brace / Diagonal Structural Member
38	
39	New ToolId: `'brace'` (hotkey: `BR`)
40	
41	Braces are diagonal members between a column/wall base point and a column/beam somewhere above.
42	
43	Data model:
44	```ts
45	{
46	  type: 'brace',
47	  id: string,
48	  startXMm, startYMm, startElevationMm: number,   // base anchor
49	  endXMm, endYMm, endElevationMm: number,          // top anchor
50	  profileId: string,                               // structural section profile
51	  materialId: string,
52	  structuralRole: 'structural',
53	}
54	```
55	
56	Grammar:
57	- Click base point (snaps to column base, floor, or arbitrary point at the active level elevation).
58	- Click top point (snaps to column top, beam, or arbitrary point — the elevation is the next level up by default, overridable in options bar).
59	- Optionally type an explicit angle.
60	
61	3D mesh (`viewport/meshBuilders.brace.ts`):
62	- A swept rectangular/circular cross-section along the diagonal line — reuse the beam sweep logic from the existing beam element mesh, since braces are geometrically similar.
63	
64	Plan symbol (`plan/bracePlanSymbol.ts`):
65	- A dashed diagonal line with a small "X" symbol mid-span (standard structural bracing symbol in plan).
66	
67	Inspector: profile, material, start/end anchor levels.
68	Tests: brace between two column positions, verify 3D diagonal geometry.
69	
70	### Sub-task G2: Beam System (Auto-Fill Structural Bay)
71	
72	A beam system fills a rectangular or polygonal bay with evenly spaced beams of the same profile.
73	
74	New ToolId: `'beam-system'` (hotkey: `BS`)
75	
76	Data model:
77	```ts
78	{
79	  type: 'beam_system',
80	  id: string,
81	  levelId: string,
82	  boundaryPoints: XY[],          // closed polygon (the bay boundary)
83	  beamDirection: number,         // angle in degrees (direction of span)
84	  spacingMm: number,             // centre-to-centre spacing
85	  profileId: string,
86	  materialId: string,
87	  justification: 'centre' | 'bearing_line_1' | 'bearing_line_2',
88	}
89	```
90	
91	Grammar:
92	- Draw a closed boundary polygon (click points, close with double-click or Enter).
93	- Options bar: spacing (default 1200mm), direction (default: longest boundary edge), profile.
94	- On completion: auto-compute the set of beams that fill the boundary at the given spacing.
95	
96	The element is stored as one `beam_system` entity. The individual beams it generates are computed at render time (not stored separately), based on the spacing and direction.
97	
98	3D mesh (`viewport/meshBuilders.beamSystem.ts`):
99	- Compute intersection of each beam line with the boundary polygon to get start/end points.
100	- Reuse beam profile sweep for each individual beam.
101	
102	Plan symbol: parallel lines at the beam spacing within the boundary + direction arrow.
103	
104	Inspector: spacing, direction, profile, boundary editing (show grips on boundary vertices).
105	
106	Tests: 4m × 6m bay, 1200mm spacing, verify 5 beams are generated; verify beams clip at boundary.
107	
108	### Sub-task G3: Column at Grid Intersections (Batch Placement)
109	
110	Revit's "At Grids" button places a column at every intersection of selected grid lines.
111	
112	- When the `column` tool is active, add an "At Grids" button to the options bar.
113	- Grammar: click "At Grids" → user selects grid lines by clicking them (multi-select, or box-select). On Enter: columns are placed at every pairwise intersection.
114	- Compute intersections: for each pair of non-parallel grid lines, solve for intersection point.
115	- Dispatch N `createColumn` commands for the N intersection points.
116	- Options bar: column type, base level, top level (same for all).
117	
118	Tests: 3 horizontal grids × 3 vertical grids → 9 columns placed at correct intersections.
119	
120	### Sub-task G4: Sloped / Inclined Columns
121	
122	Currently columns are always vertical. Add support for inclined columns.
123	
124	Data model addition:
125	```ts
126	// existing column fields +
127	topOffsetXMm: number,   // horizontal offset of the column top from its base plan position
128	topOffsetYMm: number,
129	```
130	
131	When `topOffsetXMm/YMm` are non-zero, the column is tilted.
132	
133	3D mesh: update `meshBuilders.ts` column case to extrude along the inclined axis instead of vertical.
134	
135	Plan symbol: show the column base footprint (solid) and the top footprint (dashed, offset) connected by a diagonal line.
136	
137	Inspector: top offset X/Y fields. Auto-computed angle shown as read-only.
138	
139	Tests: column with 500mm X offset, verify 3D mesh has correct top/base vertices.
140	
141	### Sub-task G5: In-Place Mass Volume Creation (Conceptual Design)
142	
143	This is the core of Chapter 11 — the top-down massing workflow.
144	
145	Revit's in-place mass lets you model building volumes from primitive shapes and combinations.
146	
147	New element type family: `mass_*`. Implement these primitive mass forms:
148	
149	#### G5a: Box Mass
150	```ts
151	{ type: 'mass_box', widthMm, depthMm, heightMm, insertionXMm, insertionYMm, baseElevationMm, rotationDeg }
152	```
153	- 3D: simple box geometry
154	- Plan: rectangle footprint + diagonal cross (standard mass convention)
155	- Grammar: click centre, drag to set width/depth (or type values in options bar)
156	
157	#### G5b: Extruded Mass
158	```ts
159	{ type: 'mass_extrusion', profilePoints: XY[], heightMm, baseElevationMm }
160	```
161	- 3D: prism shape (polygon base extruded to height)
162	- Grammar: sketch a closed polygon on the active work plane, type height in options bar
163	
164	#### G5c: Revolved Mass
165	```ts
166	{ type: 'mass_revolution', profilePoints: XY[], axisPt1: XY, axisPt2: XY, startAngleDeg, endAngleDeg, baseElevationMm }
167	```
168	- 3D: surface of revolution around the axis — use THREE.LatheGeometry
169	- Grammar: draw profile, pick axis line, set angle range
170	
171	#### G5d: Mass Tool in ToolRegistry
172	New ToolIds: `'mass-box'`, `'mass-extrusion'`, `'mass-revolution'`
173	
174	Implement all three in toolRegistry + grammar + meshBuilders.
175	
176	### Sub-task G6: Roof by Face (Mass → Roof)
177	
178	When a `mass_*` element exists, the user can select one of its faces and generate a `roof` element from it.
179	
180	- Selection action on a mass face: "Create Roof by Face" (shown in the selection toolbar when a mass face is selected in 3D).
181	- The face becomes the boundary/shape of the roof.
182	- Dispatches `{ type: 'createRoofByFace', massFaceRef: { elementId, faceIndex }, roofTypeId }`.
183	- The resulting roof element has `massFaceRef` set — it stays parametrically linked to the mass face (if the mass is resized, the roof updates).
184	- 3D: the roof mesh is built from the mass face geometry.
185	
186	Tests: create a box mass, create a roof by face on its top surface, verify roof element is created and positioned at correct elevation.
187	
188	### Sub-task G7: Wall by Face (Mass → Wall)
189	
190	Same pattern as Roof by Face but for vertical/sloped faces.
191	
192	- "Create Wall by Face" on a mass vertical face.
193	- Dispatches `{ type: 'createWallByFace', massFaceRef: { elementId, faceIndex }, wallTypeId }`.
194	- The wall follows the face boundary, top constraint follows the face top edge elevation.
195	- Tests: create box mass, create wall on each vertical face, verify 4 walls matching mass footprint.
196	
197	### Sub-task G8: Floor by Face / Body Levels
198	
199	Revit's "Floor by Face" creates floor slabs from mass faces at each level intersection.
200	
201	- "Create Floors by Level" on a selected mass element: for each project level that intersects the mass volume, create a floor element.
202	- The floor boundary is the horizontal cross-section of the mass at that elevation.
203	- Dispatches a batch of `createFloor` commands.
204	- Tests: a 10m tall box mass with 3 levels, verify 3 floor elements are created at correct elevations with the box footprint.
205	
206	### Sub-task G9: Curtain Wall Authoring UI
207	
208	The mesh builder for curtain panels exists but there is no user-facing curtain wall authoring workflow. Implement it.
209	
210	Curtain wall is a special wall type with:
211	- Grid: horizontal and vertical division lines dividing the wall face into panels
212	- Panels: infill elements (glass, solid, door) in each grid cell
213	- Mullions: profile elements at each grid line intersection and border
214	
215	Data model (extend existing wall):
216	```ts
217	// when wall.curtainWall is set:
218	curtainWall: {
219	  gridH: { count?: number, spacingMm?: number, offsets?: number[] },  // horizontal grid divisions
220	  gridV: { count?: number, spacingMm?: number, offsets?: number[] },  // vertical grid divisions
221	  defaultPanelType: string,          // 'glass' | 'opaque' | 'door' | 'empty'
222	  mullionType: string,
223	  panelOverrides: { [cellKey: string]: string }  // e.g. { '2,3': 'door' }
224	}
225	```
226	
227	UI:
228	- In the wall type picker, add a "Curtain Wall" category with preset types: "Storefront", "Curtain Wall", "Exterior Glazing".
229	- When a curtain wall is selected, the inspector shows: H grid (count/spacing), V grid (count/spacing), panel type, mullion type.
230	- "Edit Grid": in plan view, clicking a curtain wall enters a grid-edit mode where the user can click to add/remove grid lines at exact positions.
231	- "Pin Grid Line" / "Unpin Grid Line": pinned grid lines stay fixed; unpinned lines adjust when the wall is resized.
232	
233	3D rendering update in `viewport/meshBuilders.ts`: route curtain walls to the curtain panel builder instead of the solid wall builder. The existing `meshBuilders.curtainPanels.test.ts` should guide the implementation.
234	
235	Plan rendering: curtain walls show as a thin line with short perpendicular tick marks for each grid division.
236	
237	Tests:
238	- `meshBuilders.curtainWall.test.ts`: 4m × 3m curtain wall, 3 vertical divisions, 2 horizontal → 6 glass panels rendered
239	- `curtainWallPlanSymbol.test.ts`: verify plan projection shows the grid tick marks
240	
241	---
242	
243	## Definition of Done
244	
245	For each sub-task:
246	- TypeScript compiles without errors
247	- ≥2 unit tests per new module
248	- 3D mesh visible in viewport, plan symbol visible in plan
249	- No regressions in existing structural or mass mesh tests
250	- Tracker entry updated in `spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md`

file.content:
# WP-G — Structural Completion & Conceptual Massing

## Context

You are an orchestrating engineer on the bim-ai repository (`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`).
bim-ai is a browser-based BIM authoring tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest).

Repo layout (critical paths):
- `packages/web/src/tools/toolRegistry.ts` — ToolId union + TOOL_REGISTRY (add: brace, beam-system, column-at-grids, mass tools)
- `packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.ts` — 3D mesh dispatcher (add entries for new element types)
- `packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.mass.ts` — EXISTING mass mesh builder (study this first)
- `packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.mass.test.ts` — mass tests
- `packages/web/src/plan/planProjection.ts` — 2D projection (add mass footprints, beam system lines)
- `packages/web/src/plan/planElementMeshBuilders.ts` — 2D plan element renderers
- `packages/core/src/` — shared types (Element, XY, etc.)

Architecture patterns:
- All new elements follow the same pattern: define element shape in core types, implement 3D mesh builder in `viewport/meshBuilders.<type>.ts`, implement 2D plan symbol in `plan/<type>PlanSymbol.ts`, add to `meshBuilders.ts` dispatcher and `planProjection.ts`.
- Semantic commands: study `{ type: 'createWall', ... }` and `{ type: 'createRoof', ... }` for command shape conventions.
- Structural elements have `structuralRole: 'structural' | 'non-structural'` on their data.
- Prettier runs automatically. Tests: `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web`.

## Safe Parallel Work Rules

1. `git pull --rebase origin main` before starting each sub-task.
2. Commit + push after every completed sub-feature.
3. `git pull --rebase origin main && git push origin main` after each push.
4. Do NOT touch: annotation/dim files, stair/ramp (WP-C), export (WP-E), phase dialogs (WP-F), groups (WP-B), view/sheet rendering (WP-D).
5. `toolRegistry.ts` is shared — always rebase before editing.

## Your Mission

Implement structural completion (beam systems, braces, column grid placement, sloped columns) and the conceptual massing / top-down design workflow. Covers tracker Chapters 9 and 11 entirely, plus parts of 8. Dispatch sub-agents for independent sub-tasks.

---

### Sub-task G1: Brace / Diagonal Structural Member

New ToolId: `'brace'` (hotkey: `BR`)

Braces are diagonal members between a column/wall base point and a column/beam somewhere above.

Data model:
```ts
{
  type: 'brace',
  id: string,
  startXMm, startYMm, startElevationMm: number,   // base anchor
  endXMm, endYMm, endElevationMm: number,          // top anchor
  profileId: string,                               // structural section profile
  materialId: string,
  structuralRole: 'structural',
}
```

Grammar:
- Click base point (snaps to column base, floor, or arbitrary point at the active level elevation).
- Click top point (snaps to column top, beam, or arbitrary point — the elevation is the next level up by default, overridable in options bar).
- Optionally type an explicit angle.

3D mesh (`viewport/meshBuilders.brace.ts`):
- A swept rectangular/circular cross-section along the diagonal line — reuse the beam sweep logic from the existing beam element mesh, since braces are geometrically similar.

Plan symbol (`plan/bracePlanSymbol.ts`):
- A dashed diagonal line with a small "X" symbol mid-span (standard structural bracing symbol in plan).

Inspector: profile, material, start/end anchor levels.
Tests: brace between two column positions, verify 3D diagonal geometry.

### Sub-task G2: Beam System (Auto-Fill Structural Bay)

A beam system fills a rectangular or polygonal bay with evenly spaced beams of the same profile.

New ToolId: `'beam-system'` (hotkey: `BS`)

Data model:
```ts
{
  type: 'beam_system',
  id: string,
  levelId: string,
  boundaryPoints: XY[],          // closed polygon (the bay boundary)
  beamDirection: number,         // angle in degrees (direction of span)
  spacingMm: number,             // centre-to-centre spacing
  profileId: string,
  materialId: string,
  justification: 'centre' | 'bearing_line_1' | 'bearing_line_2',
}
```

Grammar:
- Draw a closed boundary polygon (click points, close with double-click or Enter).
- Options bar: spacing (default 1200mm), direction (default: longest boundary edge), profile.
- On completion: auto-compute the set of beams that fill the boundary at the given spacing.

The element is stored as one `beam_system` entity. The individual beams it generates are computed at render time (not stored separately), based on the spacing and direction.

3D mesh (`viewport/meshBuilders.beamSystem.ts`):
- Compute intersection of each beam line with the boundary polygon to get start/end points.
- Reuse beam profile sweep for each individual beam.

Plan symbol: parallel lines at the beam spacing within the boundary + direction arrow.

Inspector: spacing, direction, profile, boundary editing (show grips on boundary vertices).

Tests: 4m × 6m bay, 1200mm spacing, verify 5 beams are generated; verify beams clip at boundary.

### Sub-task G3: Column at Grid Intersections (Batch Placement)

Revit's "At Grids" button places a column at every intersection of selected grid lines.

- When the `column` tool is active, add an "At Grids" button to the options bar.
- Grammar: click "At Grids" → user selects grid lines by clicking them (multi-select, or box-select). On Enter: columns are placed at every pairwise intersection.
- Compute intersections: for each pair of non-parallel grid lines, solve for intersection point.
- Dispatch N `createColumn` commands for the N intersection points.
- Options bar: column type, base level, top level (same for all).

Tests: 3 horizontal grids × 3 vertical grids → 9 columns placed at correct intersections.

### Sub-task G4: Sloped / Inclined Columns

Currently columns are always vertical. Add support for inclined columns.

Data model addition:
```ts
// existing column fields +
topOffsetXMm: number,   // horizontal offset of the column top from its base plan position
topOffsetYMm: number,
```

When `topOffsetXMm/YMm` are non-zero, the column is tilted.

3D mesh: update `meshBuilders.ts` column case to extrude along the inclined axis instead of vertical.

Plan symbol: show the column base footprint (solid) and the top footprint (dashed, offset) connected by a diagonal line.

Inspector: top offset X/Y fields. Auto-computed angle shown as read-only.

Tests: column with 500mm X offset, verify 3D mesh has correct top/base vertices.

### Sub-task G5: In-Place Mass Volume Creation (Conceptual Design)

This is the core of Chapter 11 — the top-down massing workflow.

Revit's in-place mass lets you model building volumes from primitive shapes and combinations.

New element type family: `mass_*`. Implement these primitive mass forms:

#### G5a: Box Mass
```ts
{ type: 'mass_box', widthMm, depthMm, heightMm, insertionXMm, insertionYMm, baseElevationMm, rotationDeg }
```
- 3D: simple box geometry
- Plan: rectangle footprint + diagonal cross (standard mass convention)
- Grammar: click centre, drag to set width/depth (or type values in options bar)

#### G5b: Extruded Mass
```ts
{ type: 'mass_extrusion', profilePoints: XY[], heightMm, baseElevationMm }
```
- 3D: prism shape (polygon base extruded to height)
- Grammar: sketch a closed polygon on the active work plane, type height in options bar

#### G5c: Revolved Mass
```ts
{ type: 'mass_revolution', profilePoints: XY[], axisPt1: XY, axisPt2: XY, startAngleDeg, endAngleDeg, baseElevationMm }
```
- 3D: surface of revolution around the axis — use THREE.LatheGeometry
- Grammar: draw profile, pick axis line, set angle range

#### G5d: Mass Tool in ToolRegistry
New ToolIds: `'mass-box'`, `'mass-extrusion'`, `'mass-revolution'`

Implement all three in toolRegistry + grammar + meshBuilders.

### Sub-task G6: Roof by Face (Mass → Roof)

When a `mass_*` element exists, the user can select one of its faces and generate a `roof` element from it.

- Selection action on a mass face: "Create Roof by Face" (shown in the selection toolbar when a mass face is selected in 3D).
- The face becomes the boundary/shape of the roof.
- Dispatches `{ type: 'createRoofByFace', massFaceRef: { elementId, faceIndex }, roofTypeId }`.
- The resulting roof element has `massFaceRef` set — it stays parametrically linked to the mass face (if the mass is resized, the roof updates).
- 3D: the roof mesh is built from the mass face geometry.

Tests: create a box mass, create a roof by face on its top surface, verify roof element is created and positioned at correct elevation.

### Sub-task G7: Wall by Face (Mass → Wall)

Same pattern as Roof by Face but for vertical/sloped faces.

- "Create Wall by Face" on a mass vertical face.
- Dispatches `{ type: 'createWallByFace', massFaceRef: { elementId, faceIndex }, wallTypeId }`.
- The wall follows the face boundary, top constraint follows the face top edge elevation.
- Tests: create box mass, create wall on each vertical face, verify 4 walls matching mass footprint.

### Sub-task G8: Floor by Face / Body Levels

Revit's "Floor by Face" creates floor slabs from mass faces at each level intersection.

- "Create Floors by Level" on a selected mass element: for each project level that intersects the mass volume, create a floor element.
- The floor boundary is the horizontal cross-section of the mass at that elevation.
- Dispatches a batch of `createFloor` commands.
- Tests: a 10m tall box mass with 3 levels, verify 3 floor elements are created at correct elevations with the box footprint.

### Sub-task G9: Curtain Wall Authoring UI

The mesh builder for curtain panels exists but there is no user-facing curtain wall authoring workflow. Implement it.

Curtain wall is a special wall type with:
- Grid: horizontal and vertical division lines dividing the wall face into panels
- Panels: infill elements (glass, solid, door) in each grid cell
- Mullions: profile elements at each grid line intersection and border

Data model (extend existing wall):
```ts
// when wall.curtainWall is set:
curtainWall: {
  gridH: { count?: number, spacingMm?: number, offsets?: number[] },  // horizontal grid divisions
  gridV: { count?: number, spacingMm?: number, offsets?: number[] },  // vertical grid divisions
  defaultPanelType: string,          // 'glass' | 'opaque' | 'door' | 'empty'
  mullionType: string,
  panelOverrides: { [cellKey: string]: string }  // e.g. { '2,3': 'door' }
}
```

UI:
- In the wall type picker, add a "Curtain Wall" category with preset types: "Storefront", "Curtain Wall", "Exterior Glazing".
- When a curtain wall is selected, the inspector shows: H grid (count/spacing), V grid (count/spacing), panel type, mullion type.
- "Edit Grid": in plan view, clicking a curtain wall enters a grid-edit mode where the user can click to add/remove grid lines at exact positions.
- "Pin Grid Line" / "Unpin Grid Line": pinned grid lines stay fixed; unpinned lines adjust when the wall is resized.

3D rendering update in `viewport/meshBuilders.ts`: route curtain walls to the curtain panel builder instead of the solid wall builder. The existing `meshBuilders.curtainPanels.test.ts` should guide the implementation.

Plan rendering: curtain walls show as a thin line with short perpendicular tick marks for each grid division.

Tests:
- `meshBuilders.curtainWall.test.ts`: 4m × 3m curtain wall, 3 vertical divisions, 2 horizontal → 6 glass panels rendered
- `curtainWallPlanSymbol.test.ts`: verify plan projection shows the grid tick marks

---

## Definition of Done

For each sub-task:
- TypeScript compiles without errors
- ≥2 unit tests per new module
- 3D mesh visible in viewport, plan symbol visible in plan
- No regressions in existing structural or mass mesh tests
- Tracker entry updated in `spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md`

# WP-A Resume Prompt — Annotations & Dimensions

You are resuming a crashed agent session. Read the full original spec at
`spec/revit-parity/agent-prompts/WP-A-annotations-and-dimensions.prompt.md`
for architecture patterns and the definition of done. This prompt tells you
what was already done and what still needs implementing.

## State of the repo when you pick up

Run `git pull --rebase origin main` first. There are staged changes not yet
committed — **do not discard them**. The staged work in `toolRegistry.ts`
added several ToolIds (for WP-G). Unrelated to you; don't touch it.

## What was completed before the crash

The previous agent added ToolIds and grammar state-machine shells for the
following tools (all marked **Partial** in the tracker — grammar only, no
full renderers/persistence/grips/inspector yet):

- A1: `text` (hotkey TX) — grammar: idle→typing→commitText
- A2: `leader-text` (hotkey LT) — grammar: idle→anchor→elbow→typing→commitLeader
- A4: `angular-dimension` (hotkey AD) — grammar + plan renderer (two radial lines + angle label)
- A5: `radial-dimension` (RD) + `diameter-dimension` (DD) — grammar + basic plan renderer
- A6: `arc-length-dimension` (ALD) — grammar + midpoint label renderer
- A7: `spot-elevation` (SE) — grammar + elevation label renderer; 3D label NOT done
- A8: `spot-coordinate` (SP) — grammar + N/E label renderer
- A9: `slope-annotation` (SL) — two-click grammar + % label renderer
- A10: `material-tag` (MT) — grammar + label renderer; live layer lookup NOT done
- A12: `north-arrow` (NA) — grammar + core element type; sheet canvas rendering NOT done

**Committed but not yet done:**
`packages/web/src/plan/detailCallout.test.ts` is an untracked file —
check if it is a leftover test stub for A-something or WP-D4; commit or
delete it as appropriate after reading it.

## What still needs implementing (in priority order)

### Immediate: commit the in-flight staged work first

Before doing any new work, commit the currently staged changes:
```
git add packages/web/src/tools/toolRegistry.ts packages/core/src/index.ts \
  spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md
git commit -m "feat(ann): scaffold annotation ToolIds + grammar state machines (WP-A partial)"
git push origin main
```

### A1 / A2 — complete text and leader-text tools

The grammar exists. Still needed:
- Persistent element storage: `text_annotation` and `leader_annotation` types in core
- Full plan renderers: `plan/textAnnotationRender.ts`, `plan/leaderAnnotationRender.ts`
- Grip providers (move text box, rotation handle for text)
- Inspector: content (editable), textStyleId dropdown, rotationDeg
- At least 2 unit tests each

### A3 — Aligned Permanent Dimension completeness pass

This sub-task was not started at all. Implement:
- Permanent (non-auto) dimensions: when the `dimension` tool finalises (double-click
  or Enter), mark the element `autoGenerated: false`
- Dimension text draggable via grip on label position
- EQ constraint button: when permanent dimension chain selected, show "EQ" chip;
  clicking drives all segment lengths equal via batch move commands
- `DimensionStyle` type: textSizeMm, witnessLineGapMm, arrowStyle, textPosition
- Style picker in the annotate ribbon

### A4–A6 — elevate from Partial to Done

Each has a grammar + basic renderer but is missing:
- Persistent element type in `packages/core/src/index.ts` (if not already added)
- Grip providers (move label)
- Inspector showing relevant properties
- ≥2 tests per tool

### A7 — spot elevation 3D label

The plan renderer exists. Add a text label at world position in
`viewport/text3dGeometry.ts` (or nearest equivalent).

### A10 — material-tag live layer lookup

The grammar + renderer exist. Wire up the click → host element → material
layer lookup. The clicked element's `layerIndex` must resolve to a material
name from the wall type layer stack.

### A11 — Dimension text override (not started)

On any permanent dimension element add `textPrefix`, `textSuffix`,
`textOverride` in the inspector. When `textOverride` is set, show it instead
of the computed measurement.

### A12 — north arrow sheet rendering (not started)

The element type exists. Implement:
- `workspace/sheets/northArrowSheetRender.ts` — renders the symbol on the
  sheet canvas
- Read `truNorthAngleDeg` from the project's georeference data

## Rules

- `git pull --rebase origin main` before every major sub-task
- Commit + push after each completed sub-task
- Do NOT touch stair, roof, export, phases, structural, or groups files
- `toolRegistry.ts` is shared — always rebase before editing it
- Run `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web` before each push
- Update `spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md` as you complete items

# WP-B Resume Prompt — Modify Toolset Completion & Model Groups

You are resuming a crashed agent session. Read the full original spec at
`spec/revit-parity/agent-prompts/WP-B-modify-toolset-and-groups.prompt.md`
for architecture patterns and the definition of done. This prompt tells you
what was already done and what still needs implementing.

## State of the repo when you pick up

Run `git pull --rebase origin main` first. There are staged changes in
`toolRegistry.ts` from another WP (brace tool for WP-G) — do not modify
those lines.

## What was completed before the crash

- **B4 Done** — `createSimilar` helper + tests committed (`feat(wp-b4)`)
- **B5 Partial** — array tool math helpers (linear + radial) committed
  (`feat(wp-b5)`), but the full `'array'` ToolId entry and options bar UI
  were not wired up — verify by checking if `'array'` exists in
  `toolRegistry.ts`
- **B7 Partial** — `joinGeometry`/`unjoinGeometry` command helpers + tests
  committed (`feat(wp-b7)`); toolbar/inspector UI wiring still needed
- **ToolId scaffold** — `'scale'`, `'array'`, `'place-group'` ToolIds were
  scaffolded in toolRegistry (`feat(wp-b): scaffold new ToolIds`)

## What still needs implementing (in priority order)

### B1 — Scale Tool (grammar + command handler)

The `'scale'` ToolId exists. Still needed:
- Full grammar in `tools/scale.ts`: select elements → pick origin →
  numeric factor OR graphical two-click reference length
- Semantic command handler: `scaleElement` — for walls scale endpoints
  relative to origin; for family instances scale insertion point + size params
- Live canvas preview of scaled geometry during input
- Tests: scale a wall from origin, verify endpoints; scale a door instance

### B2 — Model Groups (full implementation)

Not started. This is the largest remaining task. Implement:
- Element types `group_definition` and `group_instance` in `packages/core/src/`
- All 5 semantic commands: `createGroup`, `placeGroup`, `ungroupElements`,
  `editGroup`/`finishEditGroup`, `renameGroup`
- Group edit mode (ghost all non-group elements, only group members editable)
- `'place-group'` tool grammar (ToolId already scaffolded)
- Plan renderer `plan/groupInstanceRender.ts` — dashed boundary when selected
- 3D renderer `viewport/groupInstance3d.ts`
- Context action "Create Group" when ≥2 elements are selected
- Inspector on group instance: name, rotation, Edit Group, Ungroup, Select All
- Project browser "Groups" subtree
- Tests: create group from 2 walls, place second instance, ungroup one

### B3 — Clipboard Paste Aligned to Selected Levels

Not started. Implement:
- `copyToLevels` command: `{ type: 'copyElementsToLevels', elementIds,
  sourceLevelId, targetLevelIds }`
- UI: "Copy to Levels…" button in selection toolbar → level picker dialog
  with checkboxes → dispatch command
- For walls: rebased baseLevelId; for floors: levelId updated; for
  doors/windows: hostId updated to wall copy on that level

### B5 — complete array tool wiring

The math helpers exist. Still needed:
- If `'array'` ToolId is not yet in TOOL_REGISTRY, add it (hotkey `AR`)
- Grammar in `tools/array.ts`: select → activate → linear mode (start,
  end, count) or radial mode (centre, angle, count) via options bar toggle
- Live canvas preview
- Options bar: Number field, "Move To: 2nd / Last" toggle

### B6 — Selection Filter Dialog

Not started. Implement:
- `plan/selectionFilter.tsx`: modal listing category counts, each with
  checkbox; unchecking deselects those elements
- "Filter" chip in selection toolbar showing "N elements selected"
- `deselectByCategory` action in the selection Zustand slice
- "Select All Instances in Project" context action

### B7 — complete join/unjoin UI wiring

Helpers + tests exist. Still needed:
- Selection action "Join" (shown when ≥2 solid elements selected) dispatching
  `joinGeometry`
- Selection action "Unjoin"
- Join glyph rendered at the junction in plan view

### B8 — Pin/Unpin toolbar shortcut

Partially done (inspector already has it). Still needed:
- Keyboard shortcut `PN` → pins all selected elements
- "Unpin All" button in selection toolbar
- Padlock icon glyph in plan canvas for pinned elements (check
  `PlanCanvas.padlock.test.tsx` — complete the rendering)

## Rules

- `git pull --rebase origin main` before every major sub-task
- Commit + push after each completed sub-task
- Do NOT touch stair/ramp, annotation/dimension, export, phase, structural,
  or massing files
- `toolRegistry.ts` is shared — always rebase before editing it
- Run `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web` before each push
- Update `spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md` as you complete items

# WP-C Resume Prompt — Vertical Circulation, Ramp & Level Management

You are resuming a crashed agent session. Read the full original spec at
`spec/revit-parity/agent-prompts/WP-C-vertical-circulation-and-levels.prompt.md`
for architecture patterns and the definition of done. This prompt tells you
what was already done and what still needs implementing.

## State of the repo when you pick up

Run `git pull --rebase origin main` first. No WP-C specific commits are
visible in the git log — the previous session likely crashed before completing
any sub-task. Start from the beginning of the original spec.

## What was completed before the crash

Nothing was committed for WP-C. All 7 sub-tasks need to be implemented.

Check `packages/web/src/levels/LevelStack.tsx` and
`packages/web/src/plan/StairBySketchCanvas.tsx` to understand the current
state before writing new code.

## What needs implementing (full original scope)

### C1 — Ramp Tool (P0 gap — do this first)

New ToolId `'ramp'` (hotkey RA). Data model, grammar (By Length + By
Sketch modes), 3D mesh (`viewport/meshBuilders.ramp.ts`), plan symbol
(`plan/rampPlanSymbol.ts`), inspector, accessibility advisor warning if
slopePercent > 8.33. Full spec in the original WP-C prompt.

### C2 — Multi-Storey Stair as a Single Element

Add `topLevelId` + `multiStorey: true` to stair element. Update
`meshBuilders.multiRunStair.ts` to stack N levels. Plan symbol clips to
current view level. Stair schedule shows full height. Tests: 3-storey stair.

### C3 — Complex Stair Configurations

Audit and fix `StairBySketchCanvas.tsx` + `stairAutobalance.ts` for:
- L-shape (2 runs + 90° landing)
- U-shape (3 runs + 2 landings)
- Winder stairs: add `winderAtCorner: boolean` option
- Spiral stair: new type `'stair_spiral'` with centrePoint, radii,
  totalAngleDeg, treadCount — plan symbol + `meshBuilders.spiralStair.ts`

### C4 — Attach Top/Base of Walls to Roof or Floor

Selection action "Attach Top" when ≥1 wall selected. Semantic command
`attachWallTop`. Handler sets `topConstraint: { hostId, hostFace: 'bottom' }`
on wall. "Detach Top/Base" clears it. The existing CSG system handles

`spec/revit-parity/agent-prompts/WP-D-views-sheets-rcp.prompt.md` for
architecture patterns and the definition of done. This prompt tells you what
was already done and what still needs implementing.

## State of the repo when you pick up

Run `git pull --rebase origin main` first.

**There are unstaged (uncommitted) changes you must handle first:**

```
packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectBrowser.tsx   — modified (not staged)
packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectBrowser.test.tsx — modified (not staged)
```

These are partial D7 work (ceiling plan section in the project browser). Read
both files, verify the changes are correct, run the tests, then commit:

```bash
pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web -- ProjectBrowser
git add packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectBrowser.tsx \
        packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectBrowser.test.tsx
git commit -m "feat(views): add Deckenansichten section to project browser (D7 partial)"
git push origin main
```

Also check for `packages/web/src/plan/detailCallout.test.ts` — this is an
untracked file that may be a D4 stub. Read it, and if it's valid test
scaffolding, integrate it into the D4 implementation below.

## What was completed before the crash

- **D3 Done** — View Range Dialog committed (`feat(view-range)`): 4-field
  modal (topOffset, cutPlaneOffset, bottomOffset, viewDepth) with
  `resolveViewRange` helper, `isAboveCutPlane` util, and `viewDepth` core
  field. This is complete.
- **D7 Partial** — ceiling plan bucket in `ProjectBrowser.tsx` (unstaged,
  see above). The `onPropertiesView` callback prop was also added.

## What still needs implementing (in priority order)

### D7 — finish Project Browser tree enhancements

After committing the unstaged ceiling plans work, complete the remaining D7
items from the original spec:
- Sections for: Ansichten grouped by type (Grundrisse, Deckenansichten,
  3D-Ansichten, Schnitte, Ansichten), Legenden, Bauteilisten/Mengen, Pläne
  (by sheet number), Familien (by category), Gruppen (once WP-B lands)
- Views are renameable (double-click name field in browser)
- Context menu: Duplicate, Delete, Properties — wire `onPropertiesView` to
  open the view's inspector
- Active view is highlighted

### D1 — Reflected Ceiling Plan (RCP) view type

New view type `'ceiling_plan'` alongside `'floor_plan'`:
- `planProjection.ts`: when `viewType === 'ceiling_plan'`, X-axis is mirrored,
  cut plane reads from topOfLevel − ceilingCutHeight (default 100mm)
- Visible: ceilings, light fixtures, structural beams at ceiling height
- Hidden: floors, terrain, roof (unless toggled)
- View creation menu: "Reflected Ceiling Plan" option, auto-create one per level
- Plan header label "RCP - Ebene 0"
- Tests: `planProjection.ceilingPlan.test.ts`, `ceilingPlanViewHeader.test.tsx`

### D2 — Interior Elevation Placement (4-Direction Marker)

Extend `'elevation'` tool with "Interior" mode in options bar. Grammar:
click inside room → 4-direction marker appears → each arrow toggleable → Enter.
Creates 1 `interior_elevation_marker` + up to 4 `elevation_view` elements.
Marker renders in plan as square with 4 arrows. Views listed under
"Sections/Elevations" in project browser.

### D4 — Detail Callout enlarged view workflow

Complete `CalloutMarker.tsx` / `DetailRegionTool.tsx`:
- When callout placed, immediately create a `detail_view` element with
  `sourceCalloutId`, `cropMinMm/MaxMm`, `scaleFactor: 5`
- Detail view opens as a new workspace tab
- Parent plan shows "See 1/A-201" reference bubble
- Project browser: detail view listed as child of parent plan view
- Tests: create callout, verify `detail_view` created with correct crop

### D5 — Named Locked 3D Views

Extend `OrbitViewpointPersistedHud.tsx`:
- "Save 3D View As…": prompts for name, saves `saved_3d_view` element with
  camera matrix + section box state
- Saved views in project browser under "3D-Ansichten"
- Open (restore camera), duplicate, rename, delete
- Placeable on sheet as viewport
- Lock toggle: padlock icon in 3D header when locked

### D6 — Sheet Revision Management

- `revision` + `sheet_revision` + `revision_cloud` element types in core
- "Manage Revisions" button in sheet properties panel
- "Revisions on Sheet" in sheet inspector
- Revision table in sheet title block (Rev#, Date, Description columns)
- `'revision-cloud'` ToolId: draw closed boundary, renders as scalloped curve
- Tests: create revision, apply to sheet, verify table renders

### D8 — Color Fill Scheme Dialog

- "Color Scheme…" button in plan header or annotate ribbon
- Dialog: category picker + color per row
- Dispatch `applyColorScheme` command
- Plan renderer: 30% opacity fill per room
- Color fill legend element `plan/colorFillLegend.ts`
- Tests: 3 rooms, verify correct fill colors

## Rules

- `git pull --rebase origin main` before every major sub-task
- Commit + push after each completed sub-task
- Do NOT touch stair/ramp, annotation tools, export pipeline, phase dialogs,
  structural, or massing files
- `toolRegistry.ts` is shared — always rebase before editing
- Run `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web` before each push
- Update `spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md` as you complete items

# WP-E Resume Prompt — Export Pipeline & Interoperability

You are resuming a crashed agent session. Read the full original spec at
`spec/revit-parity/agent-prompts/WP-E-export-and-interop.prompt.md` for
architecture patterns and the definition of done. This prompt tells you what
was already done and what still needs implementing.

## State of the repo when you pick up

Run `git pull --rebase origin main` first. No uncommitted WP-E changes exist —
all prior work was committed. The `packages/web/src/export/` directory already
exists from earlier work.

## What was completed before the crash

- **E1 Partial** — IFC 2x3 STEP exporter committed (`feat(export): IFC 2x3
  STEP exporter (E1)`); tracker marks it Partial — check what geometry types
  are missing and fill gaps (rooms as IfcSpace, openings, material layers)
- **E3 Done** — CSV exporter committed (`feat(export): client-side CSV
  exporter + clipboard copy for schedules (E3)`). This is complete.
- **E4 Done** — PDF export from sheets committed (`docs(tracker): mark
  §12.4.5 PDF-Export as Done — P1`). This is complete.
- **E5 Partial** — Linked model tracker entry updated to Partial (`docs(spec):
  update §12.1.1 Verknüpfungen — link_model workflow is Partial (E5)`).
  Implementation may be partial; verify `linkedGhosting.ts` exists and the
  `linked_model` element type is in core.
- **E6 Done** — DXF underlay completion pass committed: circle/text/hatch
  entity rendering added. This is complete.

## What still needs implementing (in priority order)

### E2 — DWG/DXF Export (not started, highest remaining priority)

This is the most important missing item. Create
`packages/web/src/export/dxfExporter.ts` using the `dxf-writer` npm package.

Layer mapping (Revit-compatible):
- Walls → `A-WALL`, Doors → `A-DOOR`, Windows → `A-GLAZ`, Columns → `A-COLS`
- Stairs → `A-STRS`, Annotations → `A-ANNO`, Dimensions → `A-ANNO-DIMS`
- Rooms → `A-AREA`, Grid lines → `S-GRID`, Reference planes → `A-REFP`

For each plan view being exported:
- Walls: pairs of parallel polylines (inner + outer face)
- Doors: arc (door swing) + line (door leaf)
- Windows: 3 lines (frame outline, glass line)
- Rooms: closed polyline on A-AREA layer
- Dimensions: DXF DIMENSION entities
- Grids: CIRCLE + LINE entities
- Text annotations: TEXT entities

UI: File menu "Export" → "DXF/DWG…" → dialog (views to export, units mm/m,
layer naming). Download as `ProjectName_Grundriss_EG.dxf`.

Tests:
- `dxfExporter.test.ts`: 4-wall plan → DXF contains POLYLINE on `A-WALL`
- Layer assignment for door and window elements

### E1 — complete IFC 2x3 export gaps

Check `packages/web/src/export/ifcExporter.ts` for what's missing:
- Rooms as `IfcSpace` with floor area property
- `IfcOpeningElement` + `IfcRelVoidsElement` for doors/windows in host walls
- Material layer export: `IfcMaterialLayerSetUsage` for layered walls
- Per-element `IfcPropertySet` (Pset_WallCommon, Pset_DoorCommon, etc.)
- Round-trip parse check: verify exported IFC can be parsed back by web-ifc

### E5 — complete Linked Model implementation

Check what exists for `linked_model`. Still needed:
- `linked_model` element type in `packages/core/src/` (if missing)
- "Insert → Link bim-ai Model" UI: project picker → creates `linked_model`
- Plan view: ghosted rendering (reuse `linkedGhosting.ts`)
- 3D: ghosted treatment
- Inspector: URL, offset, rotation, visibility toggle, Unload, Remove
- Linked model elements must NOT be selectable/editable
- Tests: `linkedModelGhosting.test.ts`

## Rules

- `git pull --rebase origin main` before every major sub-task
- Commit + push after each completed sub-task
- Do NOT touch stair/ramp, annotation tools, phase dialogs, structural,
  massing, or view/sheet rendering
- Run `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web` before each push
- Update `spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md` as you complete items

# WP-F Resume Prompt — Phases, Project Configuration & Templates

You are resuming a crashed agent session. Read the full original spec at
`spec/revit-parity/agent-prompts/WP-F-phases-and-project-config.prompt.md`
for architecture patterns and the definition of done. This prompt tells you
what was already done and what still needs implementing.

## State of the repo when you pick up

Run `git pull --rebase origin main` first. No uncommitted WP-F changes exist.

## What was completed before the crash

- **F5 Done** — Project templates + template picker at project creation
  committed (`feat(templates): F5 project templates + template picker at
  project creation`). This is complete; tracker was updated.

All other sub-tasks (F1–F4, F6, F7) need to be implemented.

## What still needs implementing (in priority order)

### F1 — Full Phase Management Dialog (highest priority)

Currently phases filter rendering but users cannot create/delete/rename them.

Data model: `phase` element `{ id, name, sequenceNumber, description }`.
Default phases: Bestand (1), Abriss (2), Neubau (3).

Semantic commands: `createPhase`, `deletePhase`, `renamePhase`,
`reorderPhase`, `setElementPhase`.

UI — `packages/web/src/phases/PhaseManagerDialog.tsx`:
- Table of phases: Sequence, Name, Description columns
- Add, delete, rename, reorder via drag-and-drop
- Warning on delete if phase has elements

Inspector integration: every element shows a "Phase" dropdown.
Active phase in `PhaseDropdown.tsx` (new elements go into this phase).

### F7 — Visibility/Graphics Override Dialog (V/G) (high value)

Create `packages/web/src/workspace/visibilityGraphicsDialog/VisibilityGraphicsDialog.tsx`.
Shortcut `VV` opens it from any plan/section view.

Table of model categories (Walls, Doors, Windows, Floors, Roofs, Stairs,
Columns, Grids, Reference Planes, Rooms, etc.).
Each row: Visible checkbox, Line Colour, Line Weight, Fill Pattern, Halftone.

Store overrides in view element:
```ts
visibilityOverrides: { [category: string]: { visible: boolean, lineColor: string,
  lineWeight: number, fillPattern: string | null, halftone: boolean } }
```

Thread through `planProjection.ts`. Start with just Visible + Line Colour +
Halftone (pattern/weight are bonus).

Tests: hide Grids → verify grid elements not rendered; halftone walls → verify opacity.

### F2 — Phase Graphic Overrides
