111	export function buildPrimaryNavigationSections(
112	  elementsById: Record<string, Element>,
113	): LeftRailSection[] {
114	  const all = Object.values(elementsById) as Element[];
115	  const levels = all
116	    .filter((e): e is Extract<Element, { kind: 'level' }> => e.kind === 'level')
117	    .sort((a, b) => a.elevationMm - b.elevationMm);
118	  const allPlanViews = all.filter(
119	    (e): e is Extract<Element, { kind: 'plan_view' }> => e.kind === 'plan_view',
120	  );
121	  const planViews = allPlanViews.filter((p) => p.planViewSubtype !== 'area_plan');
122	  const viewpoints = all.filter(
123	    (e): e is Extract<Element, { kind: 'viewpoint' }> => e.kind === 'viewpoint',
124	  );
125	  const savedViews = all.filter(
126	    (e): e is Extract<Element, { kind: 'saved_view' }> => e.kind === 'saved_view',
127	  );
128	  const sections = all.filter(
129	    (e): e is Extract<Element, { kind: 'section_cut' }> => e.kind === 'section_cut',
130	  );
131	  const sheets = all.filter((e): e is Extract<Element, { kind: 'sheet' }> => e.kind === 'sheet');
132	  const schedules = all.filter(
133	    (e): e is Extract<Element, { kind: 'schedule' }> => e.kind === 'schedule',
134	  );
135	  const projectSettings = all.find(
136	    (e): e is Extract<Element, { kind: 'project_settings' }> => e.kind === 'project_settings',
137	  );
138	  const levelNameById = new Map(levels.map((l) => [l.id, l.name]));
139	  return [
140	    ...(projectSettings
141	      ? [
142	          {
143	            id: 'project',
144	            label: 'Project',
145	            icon: ProjectInfoIcon,
146	            rows: [
147	              {
148	                id: projectSettings.id,
149	                label: 'Project settings',
150	                icon: ProjectInfoIcon,
151	                hint: projectSettings.displayLocale ?? 'global settings',
152	                renamable: false,
153	              },
154	            ],
155	          },
156	        ]
157	      : []),
158	    {
159	      id: 'floor-plans',
160	      label: 'Floor Plans',
161	      icon: PlanViewIcon,
162	      rows: planViews.map((p) => ({
163	        id: p.id,
164	        label: p.name,
165	        icon: PlanViewIcon,
166	        hint: `${levelNameById.get(p.levelId) ?? p.levelId}`,
167	        renamable: true,
168	      })),
169	    },
170	    {
171	      id: '3d-views',
172	      label: '3D Views',
173	      icon: OrbitViewIcon,
174	      rows: [...viewpoints, ...savedViews]
175	        .sort((a, b) => a.name.localeCompare(b.name))
176	        .map((v) => ({
177	          id: v.id,
178	          label: v.name,
179	          icon: OrbitViewIcon,
180	          hint: v.kind === 'saved_view' ? 'saved 3D snapshot' : 'saved camera',
181	          renamable: true,
182	        })),
183	    },
184	    {
185	      id: 'sections',
186	      label: 'Sections',
187	      icon: SectionViewIcon,
188	      rows: sections.map((s) => ({
189	        id: s.id,
190	        label: s.name,
191	        icon: SectionViewIcon,
192	        hint: 'cut view',
193	        renamable: true,
194	      })),
195	    },
196	    {
197	      id: 'sheets',

201	        id: s.id,

204	        hint: `${sheetIntentLabel(readSheetIntent(s))} · ${s.viewportsMm?.length ?? 0} viewports`,
205	        renamable: true,
206	      })),
207	    },

- **Global parameters** (Ch. 3.8) — not started; new concept requiring a project-level parameter table; extend `project_settings` with a `globalParams` map + dialog + formula binding
- **DWG/DGN export** (Ch. 12.4.3) — DXF export done (E2); DWG/DGN format not started

- Multiple simultaneous view windows (Ch. 1.6.12) — not started

content.text:
Both commits pushed. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

**§6.1.3 — Named Locked 3D View lock toggle:**
- `isLocked?: boolean` added to `SavedViewElem` and `UpdateSavedViewCmd` in `core/index.ts`
- "Lock Camera" / "Unlock Camera" button in `WorkspaceLeftRail.tsx` context menu (dispatches `update_saved_view` with `isLocked`)
- Same button in `ProjectBrowserV3` context menu via `onToggleLockView` prop
- Viewport.tsx lock behavior was already wired (`savedViewLocked` blocking pan/orbit) — just needed the type

**§14.6 — Walkthrough camera path:**
- `CameraPathElem`, `WalkthroughKeyframe`, `CreateCameraPathCmd` types in `core/index.ts`
- `'camera_path'` added to `ElemKind` union
- `walkthroughStateRef` in `Viewport.tsx` — left-click captures keyframe, Enter commits path, Escape resets
- `cameraPaths[]` local Zustand state (not server-persisted) + `addCameraPath` action
- "Walkthroughs" section in `WorkspaceLeftRail.tsx` with step-through playback on click
- `WalkthroughPlaybackPanel.tsx` — RAF loop with `lerpKeyframe` interpolation, Play/Pause/Reset, speed 0.5×/1×/2×, loop toggle
- **9 new tests** (6 grammar + 4 lock toggle) + 11 RAF interpolation tests (from wave-2 agent)

The tracker is updated and wave-3 agents can be scheduled normally.

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:

The session continued implementing 2 missing WP-D items from wave-2 (locked 3D view toggle and walkthrough camera path animation) so the tracker would be clean for wave-3 scheduling. After that work was committed and pushed, the user asked: "did you already write the wave-3 agent prompts? if not, do that please and give me the onliners i can use to kickoff agents ('your task is to ...')"

2. Key Technical Concepts:

- bim-ai: browser-based BIM authoring tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest) at `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`
- `SavedViewElem` — standalone exported type NOT in the `Element` inline union (lines 1122-2992 of core/index.ts); `as SavedViewElem` is an unsafe cast
- `CameraPathElem` / `WalkthroughKeyframe` / `CreateCameraPathCmd` — new types added to core/index.ts in prior session
- `reduceWalkthrough` / `WalkthroughState` / `initialWalkthroughState` in toolGrammar.ts — grammar already existed
- Camera paths stored as **client-side only** Zustand state (`cameraPaths[]` in store) — not persisted to server
- Lock toggle goes through `onSemanticCommand` → `update_saved_view` command to server

          fovDeg: 60,
          timeSec: walkthroughStateRef.current.keyframes.length * 3,

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

You are an agent working on the **bim-ai** repo (`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`).
bim-ai is a browser-based BIM authoring tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest).
This prompt is self-contained — start here.

---

## Repo orientation

```
packages/core/src/index.ts                          — Element union + ElemKind
packages/web/src/plan/PlanCanvas.tsx                — main plan interaction handler
packages/web/src/plan/gripProtocol.ts               — grip interaction protocol
packages/web/src/plan/GripLayer.tsx                 — grip rendering
packages/web/src/plan/planElementMeshBuilders.ts    — dispatches plan symbols per element
packages/web/src/plan/symbology.ts                  — plan symbol primitives
packages/web/src/tools/toolRegistry.ts              — ToolId union + TOOL_REGISTRY
packages/web/src/tools/toolGrammar.ts               — per-tool grammar state machines
```

Tests: co-located `*.test.ts` — run `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web`.
Prettier runs automatically after every Edit/Write.

---

## What wave 1 already built — DO NOT rebuild these

- `interior_elevation_marker` element type in `@bim-ai/core` with fields:
  `positionMm`, `levelId`, `radiusMm`, `elevationViewIds` (N/S/E/W)
- `'interior-elevation'` tool (hotkey `IE`) in toolRegistry.ts, wired in PlanCanvas.tsx
- Plan symbol: 4-quadrant circle with inward arrows in `symbology.ts` (D2)
- A1–A12 grammar shells complete in `toolGrammar.ts`
- `permanent_dimension` element type in `core/index.ts` with `witnessPoints`, `valueMm`

---

## Tasks

### A — Interior elevation marker inspector + drag grip

**A1. Inspector panel**: When an `interior_elevation_marker` element is selected, the
Inspector panel should render:
- `radiusMm` — number input (mm), dispatches `{ type: 'updateElement', id, patch: { radiusMm } }`
- `levelId` — dropdown from project levels (study how other element inspectors do this)

Study how `text_note` or `spot_elevation` inspector panels are structured (search the
Inspector component for existing annotation inspectors added in wave 1).

**A2. Drag grip**: In `gripProtocol.ts` (or the grip registration pattern used by wave-1
A1/A2 grips), register a grip for `interior_elevation_marker` that:
- Shows a centre-point drag handle at `positionMm` in plan
- On drag-end dispatches `{ type: 'moveElement', id, positionMm: newPos }`
  (or whatever the canonical move command is — study `moveTool.ts`)

Tests:
- Inspector renders radiusMm input for interior_elevation_marker
- Grip provider returns a centre handle at the correct position
- Dragging the grip emits the correct move command

Update tracker §6.1.5: interior elevation markers now have inspector + grip → "Partial — D2
(inspector+grip done; full elevation-view rendering is separate)"

---

### B — EQ condition on aligned dimensions (Ch. 4.2.2, P2)

**B1. Data model**: Add `eqEnabled?: boolean` to the `permanent_dimension` element type
in `core/index.ts`.

**B2. Plan rendering**: In the plan dimension renderer (find where `permanent_dimension`
elements are drawn — search `planElementMeshBuilders.ts` or `planProjection.ts`):
- When a selected `permanent_dimension` has ≥3 witness lines, render a small "EQ"
  button/label above the midpoint of the full dimension span
- When `eqEnabled: true`, display "EQ" instead of individual segment values, and
  distribute witness spacings equally (recompute positions for visual display only —
  do not move actual geometry)

**B3. Interaction**: Clicking the "EQ" label toggles `eqEnabled` via
`{ type: 'updateElement', id, patch: { eqEnabled: !current } }`.

69:#### 1.6.3 Schnellzugriff-Werkzeugkasten (quick access toolbar)
639:### 6.3 Plan mit Änderungsliste (sheet with revision table / delta list)
641:`revision` and `sheet_revision` element types added to `@bim-ai/core`. `ManageRevisionsDialog.tsx` implements CRUD for project revisions and per-sheet assignment via checkboxes (`sheet_revision` join records). Commands: `create_revision`, `update_revision`, `delete_revision`, `add_sheet_revision`, `remove_sheet_revision`. Revision clouds (`revision_cloud` annotation — ANN-03) already existed. `SheetRevisionTableSvg` renders in the bottom-right corner of the title block (via `sheetTitleblockAuthoring.tsx`); `resolveSheetRevisions` joins `sheet_revision` → `revision` records, sorts by number ascending, shows a placeholder "—" row when none are assigned. Tests: `sheetRevisionTable.test.ts` (5 tests). Revision-cloud draw tool (E3): `'revision-cloud'` ToolId (hotkey RC, plan mode), `RevisionCloudState`/`reduceRevisionCloud` grammar, PlanCanvas click/dblclick/Enter/Escape wiring, and `revisionCloudPlanThree` plan renderer (dashed closed polygon, orange default, view-scoped). Tests in `toolGrammar.revisionCloud.test.ts` (5) and `revisionCloudRendering.test.ts` (7).

input.description: Check §8.1.4 and §8.1.5 tracker status

content.content:
304:F2 (Phase Graphic Overrides): `phaseFilterMode` added to plan_view element type in core/index.ts. `resolvePhaseGraphicStyle()` pure function added to planProjection.ts — given a view's phaseId, phaseFilterMode, and an element's phaseCreated/phaseDemolished, returns `{hidden, opacity, dashed, grey}`. rebuildPlanMeshes() in symbology.ts extended with `viewPhaseId` + `phaseFilterMode` opts; `applyPhaseStyle()` helper traverses Three.js object children and applies grey colouring + opacity + LineDashedMaterial for demolished elements. Phase overlay applied to walls, curtain walls, doors, windows, floors. PlanCanvas.tsx derives viewPhaseId/phaseFilterMode from active plan_view element and passes to rebuildPlanMeshes. Phase filter mode selector (dropdown) added to Workspace.tsx paneTrailingControls (visible when active plan view has a phaseId set); dispatches updateElementProperty to persist the mode. Modes: `new_construction` (existing=grey, demolished=hidden, new=normal), `demolition` (existing=grey, demolished=dashed, new=hidden), `existing` (only existing shown), `as_built` (all normal). 11 tests added to planProjection.test.ts (all pass).
713:#### 8.1.4 Fassadenwände (curtain walls: grid, panels, mullions)
715:Implemented — inspector + custom grid editing done. Panel grid rendering: done (`meshBuilders.curtainPanels.test.ts`). Plan symbol: done (`curtainWallPlanSymbol.ts`, 4 tests). Inspector extended with H/V count inputs, Panel type dropdown (Glass/Spandrel/Solid), Mullion type dropdown (Rectangular/Circular/None), and "Edit Grid…" button; `customVDivisions` field added to `curtainWallData` type; `curtainWallPlanSymbol.ts` renders custom ticks in priority over uniform grid; `curtainWallPanelType`/`curtainWallMullionType` fields added to Python `WallElem` with `updateElementProperty` handlers; 5 new `curtainWallPlanSymbol.customDivisions.test.ts` tests pass.
910:Full top-down massing → BIM workflow (mass → generate walls/roof/floors from faces, apply curtain system to face) is Not Started end-to-end.
1141:- **Curtain wall interactive grid editing** (Ch. 8.1.4) — data model (`curtainWallData`) + plan symbol (tick marks) done; **missing**: "Edit Grid" mode, inspector H/V grid controls, panel/mullion dropdowns
---
717:#### 8.1.5 Abziehbilder (decals / surface images on wall faces)
718:**Status: Implemented — 'decal' ToolId + grammar + inspector**
719:`'decal'` added to `ToolId` union (hotkey `DC`, 3D mode only) in `toolRegistry.ts`. `DecalState` / `reduceDecal` added to `toolGrammar.ts`: idle → face-click → picking-image → image-chosen → createDecal effect (positionMm, normalVec, imageSrc, widthMm 1000, heightMm 1000). `DecalElem` extended with optional placement fields (`positionMm`, `normalVec`, `imageSrc`, `widthMm`, `heightMm`). `buildDecalMesh()` already exists in `meshBuilders.ts`. 6 grammar tests passing.
1153:- Decal placement tool (Ch. 8.1.5) — `DecalElem` type + `buildDecalMesh()` exist; need `'decal'` ToolId in toolRegistry + click-to-place grammar + inspector (image picker, scale, rotation)

input.description: Check wall parts and sloped column tracker status

content.content:
97:bim-ai has a ToolPalette with hotkeys, OptionsBar, and ToolModifierBar. Revit-style "tool stays active until escape/finish" grammar is implemented. The chained-placement model (wall chain, stair by component, etc.) is done. Missing: the full floating options bar that changes per-tool is only partially implemented for some tools.
101:bim-ai has the Inspector panel for element properties. Instance properties are shown and editable for walls, doors, windows, roofs, floors. Type properties and type duplication (WorkspaceRightRailTypeCommands.ts exists) are partially implemented. Creating entirely new types from scratch (e.g. new wall type with custom layer stack) is partially available in the wall type catalog. Full Revit-style "Typ bearbeiten" dialog with all type parameters is not yet fully parity.
161:Double-clicking to enter element-specific edit mode (e.g. Edit Profile for walls/floors, Edit Sketch for roofs) is partially implemented. Not consistently available across all element types.
256:Basic floor placement is done. Revit's alternative slab boundary methods (e.g. picking wall faces automatically to create the boundary) are partially implemented. Floor edge profile (Deckenrand) not yet supported.
